creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
On tue 28 dec 10 at 19:04:33 +0100, David Demelier <demelier.david@gmail.com>
 wrote:

> >On the contrary, I prefer that a package provides a rather complete set
> >of features, and that people with lighter needs use a port.
> >
> >Of course, if there is a real need for a minimal package, I could
> >consider a slave package, with most options deactivated by default.
> >
> >Regards,
> 
> No problem, I won't answer to people who will ask why rxvt-unicode pulls 
> 50% of gnome and firefox.
> 
>   --enable-afterimage     enable integration with libAfterImage for 
> background images
> 
> Enabling this is not default.

Well... if you read README.configure, it contains:

    --enable-afterimage (default: on)
        Add support for libAfterImage to be used for background images. It
        adds support for many file formats including JPG, PNG, SVG, TIFF,
        GIF, XPM, BMP, ICO, XCF, TGA and AfterStep image XML
        (<http://www.afterstep.org/visualdoc.php?show=asimagexml>).

        Note that with this option enabled, rxvt's memory footprint might
        increase by a few megabytes even if no extra features are used
        (mostly due to third-party libraries used by libAI). Memory
        footprint may somewhat be lowered if libAfterImage is configured
        without support for SVG.

And what about my suggestion of a slave port to build a minimal package?

Best regards,
-- 
Th. Thomas.
